# Work Packages: Charts Tab

**Inputs**: Design documents from `kitty-specs/009-charts-tab/`
**Prerequisites**: plan.md (required), spec.md (user stories), research.md, data-model.md, quickstart.md

**Tests**: Manual testing only (per constitution — no automated tests for v1).

**Organization**: Fine-grained subtasks (`Txxx`) roll up into work packages (`WPxx`). Each work package must be independently deliverable and testable.

**Prompt Files**: Each work package references a matching prompt file in `kitty-specs/009-charts-tab/tasks/` generated by `/spec-kitty.tasks`.

## Subtask Format: `[Txxx] [P?] Description`
- **[P]** indicates the subtask can proceed in parallel (different files/components).
- Paths relative to repository root.

---

## Work Package WP01: Foundation — Models, Repository Methods, ChartDataService, Dashboard Skeleton (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Create all chart data model types, add date-range query methods to SetRepository, create ChartDataService actor with method stubs, create ChartsDashboardViewModel skeleton, create ChartsDashboardView shell, and wire the charts tab into ContentView.
**Independent Test**: Charts tab appears in bottom nav. ChartsDashboardView renders with "Charts" header and section placeholders (overview + exercise list). ChartModels compile. SetRepository new methods compile. ChartDataService initializes with correct dependencies.
**Prompt**: `kitty-specs/009-charts-tab/tasks/WP01-foundation-models-service-skeleton.md`
**Estimated prompt size**: ~450 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T001 [P] Create `Reppo/Features/Charts/Models/ChartModels.swift` — all chart data types (OverviewChartData, WeeklyVolumePoint, WeeklyFrequencyPoint, MuscleGroupVolume, ExerciseCardData, TrendDirection, ExerciseDetailChartData, TopWeightPoint, RepPRProgressionData, RepSeries, RepPRPoint, TimeRange enum)
- [x] T002 [P] Add SetRepository date-range query methods — `fetchSets(from:to:)` for overview and `fetchSets(exerciseId:from:to:)` for detail charts
- [x] T003 Create `Reppo/Core/Services/ChartDataService.swift` — actor with repository dependencies and method stubs
- [x] T004 Add ChartDataService to ServiceContainer — new property, initialize in correct dependency order
- [x] T005 Create `Reppo/Features/Charts/ViewModels/ChartsDashboardViewModel.swift` — @Observable with state, ChartDataService dependency, loading stubs
- [x] T006 Create `Reppo/Features/Charts/Views/ChartsDashboardView.swift` — ScrollView with OVERVIEW and PER EXERCISE section placeholders, navigation support
- [x] T007 Wire ChartsDashboardView into `Reppo/App/ContentView.swift` — replace `ChartsPlaceholderView()`

### Implementation Notes
- T001 and T002 are independent — can be done first or in parallel.
- T003 depends on T001 (uses chart model types) and T002 (uses repository method signatures).
- T004 depends on T003 (adds the service to the container).
- T005 depends on T003/T004 (injects ChartDataService).
- T006 depends on T005 (uses the ViewModel).
- T007 is a small edit in ContentView.swift.
- WorkoutRepository already has `fetchWorkouts(for: ClosedRange<Date>)` — no new method needed there.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T001 and T002 are fully independent (different files).
- T003-T007 are sequential.

### Dependencies
- None (starting package).

### Risks & Mitigations
- SetRepository is a `@ModelActor` — new methods must follow the same pattern with `FetchDescriptor` + `#Predicate`.
- ChartDataService dependency injection — must match ServiceContainer pattern (protocol-based, `any ChartDataServiceProtocol`).

---

## Work Package WP02: Overview Dashboard — Weekly Volume, Training Frequency, Muscle Group Distribution (Priority: P1) 🎯 MVP

**Goal**: Implement the 3 overview chart data aggregation methods in ChartDataService and create the 3 chart view components. Wire them into ChartsDashboardView with overview loading and empty states.
**Independent Test**: Charts tab shows OVERVIEW section with weekly volume bar chart (12 weeks), training frequency chart, and muscle group distribution chart. With no workout data, shows motivational empty state. Charts render within 1 second.
**Prompt**: `kitty-specs/009-charts-tab/tasks/WP02-overview-dashboard-charts.md`
**Estimated prompt size**: ~500 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T008 Implement `ChartDataService.fetchWeeklyVolume(weeks:)` — fetch sets by date range, group by ISO week, sum effectiveWeight × reps, fill zero-value weeks
- [x] T009 Implement `ChartDataService.fetchTrainingFrequency(weeks:)` — fetch workouts by date range, group by ISO week, count per week, fill zero-value weeks
- [x] T010 Implement `ChartDataService.fetchMuscleGroupDistribution(weeks:)` — fetch sets by date range, lookup Exercise.primaryMuscle (cached), group by muscle, sum volume
- [x] T011 [P] Create `Reppo/Features/Charts/Views/Components/WeeklyVolumeChart.swift` — BarMark chart, 12-week x-axis, volume y-axis
- [x] T012 [P] Create `Reppo/Features/Charts/Views/Components/TrainingFrequencyChart.swift` — sessions per week visualization
- [x] T013 [P] Create `Reppo/Features/Charts/Views/Components/MuscleGroupDistributionChart.swift` — horizontal bar chart with MuscleGroupColors
- [x] T014 Implement `ChartsDashboardViewModel.loadOverview()` + wire overview charts into ChartsDashboardView OVERVIEW section + empty state

### Implementation Notes
- Canonical set filter for all chart aggregation: After fetching sets from the repository, apply a shared filter: `set.hasData && set.setType != .warmup && set.setType != .partial`. This MUST be applied consistently in T008, T010, and T020. Extract a `chartEligibleSets(_:)` helper in ChartDataService (added in WP01/T003) to avoid duplication.
- T008-T010 are the core aggregation logic. Each fetches bounded data and aggregates in Swift.
- T008: Must fill weeks with zero volume where no workouts occurred (chart needs continuous x-axis).
- T009: WorkoutRepository already has `fetchWorkouts(for: ClosedRange<Date>)` — use it directly.
- T010: Needs an exercise cache `[UUID: Exercise]` to avoid N+1 queries. Build once per call.
- T011-T013 are pure view components — can be developed in parallel.
- T014 ties everything together: ViewModel calls the 3 service methods, stores in `overviewData`, view renders.
- All charts follow the existing card-based styling from ExerciseChartsView (bgCard background, axis styling).
- All weight/volume values are stored in kg. Chart views MUST convert to the user's preferred unit (kg or lbs) at the display layer using the existing unit preference from Settings. Apply conversion in the chart's label formatters and y-axis annotations, not in the service or ViewModel.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T011, T012, T013 are pure view components in separate files — fully parallel.
- T008, T009, T010 are independent service method implementations.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (ChartModels, ChartDataService stubs, SetRepository methods, ViewModel, View skeleton).

### Risks & Mitigations
- ISO week calculation edge cases — use `Calendar.current.dateInterval(of: .weekOfYear, for:)` consistently.
- Zero-week filling — iterate from earliest to latest week, inserting zero entries for missing weeks.
- Muscle group color mapping — reuse `MuscleGroupColors.color(for:)` from Calendar feature (feature 008).

---

## Work Package WP03: Per-Exercise Cards — Card List with Sparklines (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Implement the exercise card data loading (with sparkline e1RM points and trend direction) and create the ExerciseChartCard component. Wire the card list into ChartsDashboardView PER EXERCISE section with navigation to detail (detail screen built in WP04).
**Independent Test**: Charts tab PER EXERCISE section shows exercise cards sorted by most recent, each displaying exercise name, current e1RM, trend direction arrow, and sparkline. Cards are tappable (navigation target is placeholder until WP04).
**Prompt**: `kitty-specs/009-charts-tab/tasks/WP03-per-exercise-cards.md`
**Estimated prompt size**: ~400 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T015 Implement `ChartDataService.fetchExerciseCardData()` — fetch ExerciseStats, get bestE1RM/lastPerformed, fetch last 8 sessions' best e1RM for sparkline per exercise, compute trend direction
- [x] T016 [P] Create `Reppo/Features/Charts/Views/Components/ExerciseChartCard.swift` — card with exercise name, current e1RM, trend direction arrow (↑/↓/→), mini sparkline (LineMark)
- [x] T017 Implement `ChartsDashboardViewModel.loadExerciseCards()` — calls service, updates exerciseCards array
- [x] T018 Wire exercise card list into ChartsDashboardView PER EXERCISE section — LazyVStack with NavigationLink per card
- [x] T019 Handle edge cases — empty exercise list state (no exercises with stats), exercises without e1RM data (duration-only exercises)

### Implementation Notes
- T015 is the heaviest subtask: fetches ExerciseStats via `fetchAll()`, then for each exercise with `lastPerformedDate != nil`, fetches recent sets to build sparkline.
- T015 sparkline: fetch sets for exerciseId sorted by date DESC, group by workoutId, take max e1RM per workout, keep last 8 sessions.
- T015 trend: compare sparkline[-1] vs sparkline[-2]. If higher → `.up`, lower → `.down`, equal → `.flat`.
- T016: Sparkline is a minimal `Chart` with `LineMark` only (no axes, no labels). Just a small trend line.
- T018: Use `LazyVStack` for the exercise list. `NavigationLink` wraps each card. Destination is placeholder `Text("Detail coming in WP04")` for now.
- T019: Duration-only exercises (trackingType != WEIGHT_REPS) have no e1RM — show "-" for e1RM, no sparkline.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T016 is a pure view component — can be developed in parallel with T015.
- T017-T019 depend on T015 and T016 being ready.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (ChartModels, ChartDataService, ViewModel, View skeleton).
- Independent of WP02 (overview charts). Can run in parallel with WP02 if the ViewModel API from WP01 is stable.

### Risks & Mitigations
- Sparkline data loading for ~50+ exercises could be slow if done sequentially — batch-load or limit to exercises with recent activity (e.g., last 6 months).
- Exercises with no sessions return empty sparkline — handled by T019 edge cases.
- Unit display: e1RM values need user's unit preference conversion in the view layer.

---

## Work Package WP04: Exercise Charts Detail — Time Range Selector + 4 Chart Types (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Build the Exercise Charts Detail screen with time range selector ([3M] [6M] [1Y] [All]), 4 chart types (e1RM trend, volume/session, top weight/session, rep PR progression), the detail ViewModel, and navigation from exercise cards. Handle edge cases (empty states, single data point, no data for period).
**Independent Test**: Tapping an exercise card navigates to Exercise Charts Detail. Time range selector switches between 3M/6M/1Y/All and all charts update. 4 charts display correctly with appropriate data. Empty states show for exercises with no history. "No data for this period" shows for empty time ranges.
**Prompt**: `kitty-specs/009-charts-tab/tasks/WP04-exercise-charts-detail.md`
**Estimated prompt size**: ~550 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T020 Implement `ChartDataService.fetchExerciseDetailCharts(exerciseId:range:)` — fetch sets for exercise in date range, compute 4 chart datasets (e1RM trend, volume/session, top weight/session, rep PR progression)
- [x] T021 [P] Create `Reppo/Features/Charts/Views/Components/TimeRangeSelector.swift` — horizontal segmented picker for [3M] [6M] [1Y] [All]
- [x] T022 Create `Reppo/Features/Charts/ViewModels/ExerciseChartsDetailViewModel.swift` — @Observable with loadCharts(), changeTimeRange(), exerciseId, selectedTimeRange
- [x] T023 [P] Create `Reppo/Features/Charts/Views/Components/TopWeightChart.swift` — LineMark chart showing max effectiveWeight per session
- [x] T024 [P] Create `Reppo/Features/Charts/Views/Components/RepPRProgressionChart.swift` — multi-line chart (1RM, 3RM, 5RM series with color coding)
- [x] T025 Create `Reppo/Features/Charts/Views/ExerciseChartsDetailView.swift` — ScrollView composing TimeRangeSelector + 4 charts, exercise name header
- [x] T026 Wire navigation from ExerciseChartCard → ExerciseChartsDetailView + handle all edge cases (empty state, single data point, no data for selected period)

### Implementation Notes
- T020: The core aggregation for detail charts. Fetch sets WHERE exerciseId AND date >= range start. Group by workoutId. For each session:
  - e1RM trend: `max(set.e1RM)` where e1RM > 0
  - Volume: `sum(effectiveWeight × reps)`
  - Top weight: `max(effectiveWeight)`
  - Rep PR progression: For reps in [1, 3, 5], find best `effectiveWeight` where `set.reps >= targetReps`
- T020: Rep PR progression builds 3 separate series. Sessions where the exercise wasn't performed at that rep count produce no point (gaps in the line are OK).
- T021: Simple horizontal stack of buttons/chips with selection state. Follows the design system's chip/badge pattern (rSm corners, accent highlight for selected).
- T022: On `changeTimeRange()`, resets chartData to nil, calls loadCharts() with new range. Shows loading state during re-fetch.
- T023/T024: Follow the exact same card-based chart styling as existing ExerciseChartsView (bgCard, axis styling, colors).
- T024: Multi-line chart uses `foregroundStyle(by: .value("Series", series.label))` for color differentiation.
- T025: Composes all pieces. Reuses e1RM and volume chart rendering patterns from existing ExerciseChartsView.
- T026: Update ExerciseChartCard's NavigationLink in ChartsDashboardView (from WP03 placeholder) to push ExerciseChartsDetailView.
- Edge cases per spec: no history → empty state, single point → show point without trend line, time range with no data → "No data for this period".
- Unit conversion: All chart y-axis labels showing weight values must respect user's unit preference. Convert effectiveWeight (kg) to display unit in chart view formatters.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T021, T023, T024 are pure view components — fully parallel.
- T022 and T025 depend on the components being ready.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (ChartModels, ChartDataService, repository methods).
- Depends on WP03 (exercise card navigation — T026 updates the NavigationLink from WP03).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Rep PR progression may have sparse data (not every session has 1-rep sets) — lines connect available points only, no interpolation.
- Time range "All" with large exercise history — still bounded per exercise (~300-600 sets max). Acceptable.
- Chart y-axis scaling when values are close — Swift Charts handles auto-scaling. Test with real data.
- Unit display: All weight charts need user's unit preference conversion at the view layer.

---

## Dependency & Execution Summary

- **Sequence**: WP01 → WP02 + WP03 (parallel) → WP04
- **Parallelization**: WP02 and WP03 can run in parallel after WP01 (they touch different parts of ChartsDashboardView — overview section vs. exercise section). WP04 depends on WP03 for navigation wiring.
- **MVP Scope**: WP01 + WP02 constitute the minimum functional charts tab (overview charts visible). WP03 adds the exercise card list. WP04 adds the drill-down detail.
